model_input:
You are a UI clicking agent. Given a screenshot of a screen, determine the pixel coordinates of the target element.

Call click_at(x, y, confidence) with the center pixel coordinates of the target element.

Call click_at().

raw_message.reasoning_content:
The user wants me to click on the button that says "CLICK ME".
Looking at the image, there is a green button with the text "CLICK ME" in the center of the screen.
The button is located roughly in the middle.
Let's estimate the coordinates.
The image dimensions are not explicitly given, but I can see the button.
It's a rectangle.
Center of the image is roughly where the button is.
Let's pinpoint the coordinates.
The button is centered horizontally and vertically.
Horizontal center: 500 / 1000 (assuming normalized coordinates)
Vertical center: 500 / 1000 (assuming normalized coordinates)
Looking at the image, it's a small green box in the middle.
X coordinate: middle of the image.
Y coordinate: middle of the image.
Let's be more precise.
The box starts around x=450 and ends around x=550.
The box starts around y=460 and ends around y=540.
Center is approx (500, 500).
